summary: Small SysDolphin baselib cleanup centered on LObj reference handling. The PR moved a duplicated object reference increment helper into include/sysdolphin/baselib/object.h as inline ref_INC, updated HSD_LObjAddCurrent to call it instead of open-coding ref_count manipulation, and preserved a jobj.c-local ref_INC_alt under NON_MATCHING constraints because inline asm still depended on the old object.h string literal placement. It also added a C NON_MATCHING version of HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType while keeping the matching asm implementation for normal builds.
what_changed: Added inline void ref_INC(void* o) to object.h. The helper NULL-checks its argument, increments HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count, and asserts that the result is not HSD_OBJ_NOREF using the existing object.h assertion text and line 0x5D.
Replaced open-coded ref_count increment logic in HSD_LObjAddCurrent with ref_INC(lobj), reducing duplicated object reference-counting code in lobj.c.

In jobj.c, kept the existing helper body only for matching builds by wrapping it in #ifndef NON_MATCHING and renaming it through #define ref_INC ref_INC_alt. A comment explains this should be removed once no inline asm needs the object.h string literal.
Preserved the global string literal char lbl_804068E4[] = "object.h" in jobj.c for inline asm compatibility.
Added a C implementation of HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType under #ifdef NON_MATCHING. The C version walks current_lights, masks flags with LOBJ_TYPE_MASK, and returns the first matching HSD_LObj or NULL.
Kept the existing asm HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType implementation for matching builds under #else/#endif.
smart_moves: Centralized the common HSD object reference increment pattern in object.h rather than continuing to duplicate ref_count manipulation in individual baselib modules.
Avoided disturbing matching-sensitive code by retaining the jobj.c-local alternate helper and the object.h string literal needed by inline asm.
Used NON_MATCHING guards to document and stage a readable C implementation of HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType without replacing the matching asm path.
Documented the compiler/codegen blocker for HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType: the Profile compiler did not generate beqlr, so the C version did not match the existing instruction sequence.
decomp_lessons: When extracting shared inline helpers into headers, check whether existing inline asm depends on nearby string literals, symbol names, or layout artifacts before deleting old local copies.
A helper can be made reusable while still preserving matching by using an alternate local name under matching-only guards, as done with ref_INC_alt in jobj.c.
For functions that are semantically understood but blocked by compiler branch generation, keep the C version under NON_MATCHING with a short explanation and decomp.me scratch link if available.
Replacing open-coded ref_count increments with a shared helper improves consistency, especially when the increment has non-obvious assertion behavior tied to HSD_OBJ_NOREF.
Header cleanup in this codebase may require preserving HAL's original awkward naming and assertion text to maintain evidence and compatibility.
naming_conventions: The new helper is named ref_INC, matching existing HAL-style mixed lowercase/uppercase naming rather than normal project style.
The temporary matching-only duplicate in jobj.c is named ref_INC_alt and reached through #define ref_INC ref_INC_alt under #ifndef NON_MATCHING.
Existing symbols preserved include HSD_LObjAddCurrent, HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType, HSD_LightID2Index, HSD_JObjResolveRefs, HSD_OBJ_NOREF, LOBJ_TYPE_MASK, current_lights, and lbl_804068E4.
Reviewer ribbanya explicitly noted the awkward HAL naming with the comment: "Awful naming, HAL."
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept asm HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType in the matching build and placed the readable C implementation behind #ifdef NON_MATCHING.
The PR notes HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType was "Broken by frank" because the Profile compiler does not generate beqlr, preventing an instruction match.
Preserved char lbl_804068E4[] = "object.h" in jobj.c because inline asm still needed the literal.
Used #ifndef NON_MATCHING around ref_INC_alt in jobj.c so the header version can exist for normal C usage while matching-sensitive assembly remains unaffected.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.context: Commented on the addition of inline void ref_INC(void* o), indicating the name mirrors original/HAL naming despite being stylistically poor.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove lbl_804068E4 or the jobj.c ref_INC_alt compatibility shim.
Search for other open-coded HSD_OBJ ref_count increments that could be replaced with ref_INC.
Check whether HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType was later matched in C after resolving the beqlr/Profile compiler issue.
Audit object.h inline helpers for HAL-style names such as iref_DEC and ref_INC and confirm assertion line numbers remain intentional.
